lines: Last 48 hours: Ellis's Argentina scraped past Cabo Verde 3–2 in extra time — three goals from Argentina against Cabo Verde, Christ alive — while Carter and The Foreman's Colombia quietly did the professional job with a 1–0 over Ghana. Egypt beat Australia on pens, which affects nobody here, but someone had to mention it.

Today's big one: Paraguay vs France in the Round of 16. Ellis has Paraguay, which is genuinely funny given she's sitting 8th with Argentina already doing the heavy lifting. Meanwhile Callum, Croft, Dene AND The Foreman all have France, so whoever goes through there is going to cause a proper points pile-up in the middle of the table.

Special mention for Dene, who at 10th place with 35pts is absolutely cooked — France is his only horse left after Senegal and Bosnia both got knocked out. He's not in a sweepstake anymore, he's in a hostage situation, and the hostage is Kylian Mbappé.

Canada vs Morocco in the other Round of 16 fixture today is basically a Butters (1st, 64pts) coronation parade — he's got Morocco AND Portugal AND Norway still in. Callum, Jim and Wilmot also have Morocco, so it's in everyone's interest to see Canada capitulate, which, let's be honest, they probably will.

Watch today: if France batter Paraguay, Callum climbs the table while Ellis watches her Paraguay side get mullered — and if Morocco beat Canada, Butters edges closer to an absolutely insufferable title win. Both things happening in the same day might actually kill Jim, who's sitting 5th going absolutely nowhere fast.
